rows.notes: Clear centered page title in distinct handwriting at top of page. No amounts on this header line.

The description is legible though slightly archaic spelling; the three numerals at extreme right read clearly as 3 (pounds), 16 (shillings), 8 (pence). Vertical alignment is consistent with the rulings and these values pass currency validation.

Description read as 'Coll. Aedis.ti' (abbreviated). Amounts aligned to right read as 0 pounds, 14 shillings, 6 pence; digits are clear but the exact punctuation/abbreviation in the description is slightly uncertain.

rows.description: Mayor's capons P Mr Well mayor
rows.notes: Handwriting of description is moderately ambiguous ('Mayor's capons P Mr Well mayor' is an interpretation). The right-hand numbers read near this line as 0 pounds, 12 shillings, 6 pence, but the exact row-to-description alignment could be off by one line; chosen based on horizontal line breaks and spacing.
Description reads as 'Coll. Omnium Animar.' (abbreviated Latin). The numbers 4 / 12 / 6 at the right side align with this row and are quite clear.
Description interpreted as 'Coll. St Johans Bapt.' The right-hand figures 0 / 8 / 2 align with this line and digits are legible.
Description read as 'Coll. Balioll.' (possible spelling 'Balliol'). The amounts 0 / 12 / 6 are clearly written and aligned with this row.

Description 'Curia H[y]stings' (handwritten) is legible as a header-like entry but there are no amounts visible on the right for this line. Could be a sectional label; treated as an entry because it sits in the list.

rows.description: Procurations Kid C. Southern. morton. Michl 1712
rows.notes: Description fairly legible though abbreviated. Right-hand numbers read clearly as 1 / 19 / 0 and align with the row. All currency values within valid ranges.
Label 'Janitorii.' is legible. Amount to the right reads 1 / 14 / 8; the pence digit has slight ink smudge but alignment and surrounding rows support this reading.
Short description 'Coquo.' (cook). Amounts 1 / 10 / 4 are clear and align with ruled columns.

rows.description: Lotrici nat 20 March &c to Sept to Michl 10
rows.notes: Description is a clipped date range and is partially faded; interpreted as 'Lotrici nat 20 March...'. The amount 2 / 0 / 0 is clear at right. Some uncertainty in exact words of description.
Short description appears as 'Ponson' (or 'Ponson.'). Right-hand figures 0 / 13 / 4 are legible and well aligned.

Label 'Bibliothecario.' is legible; the numbers 0 / 13 / 4 sit on the same horizontal band as the previous line and are clearly written. There's a chance the pen-stroke alignment places the 0/13/4 on the previous or next item, but spacing suggests it belongs to this row.
Description read as 'Bede of Beggars' (handwriting is clear enough). Amount 1 / 3 / 0 is distinct and within valid ranges.

rows.description: Sullion & Quadrangle nat. Eds. Ann. Eds. (? abbreviated)
rows.notes: Description is heavily abbreviated and uncertain ('Sullion'/'Sallion' & 'Quadrangle' are tentative readings). The amount 1 / 6 / 8 is clearly present to the right; uncertainty is primarily in the wording of the description.
This line ('Dno Episcopo & Trienal Vigil.') appears as a descriptive entry but has no visible amount written on the right. Description legible but abbreviated; treated as entry with blank amounts.
Reading 'Horner West. Sup[erior?]'. The right-hand amounts 4 / 0 / 0 are large and clearly written; alignment with vertical ruling is unambiguous.
Description 'Conferaton Sermon' (conference/convocation sermon). Amount 1 / 0 / 0 is clearly written and centered on the currency columns.
Clear entry 'Curat of Southen. Ann. 1712'. The amount 10 / 0 / 0 is large, unambiguous and well aligned with the pounds column.
Description reads roughly as 'Corn pries?' (possible 'priest' or 'prize'); the amount 0 / 2 / 0 is clear. Slight uncertainty in the exact word of the description.
Description 'Synodall Aslingt. Southern Morton.' is legible though abbreviated. Amount 0 / 7 / 0 is clearly aligned and within valid range.
Parenthetical 'Castle' visible in handwriting; amount 0 / 10 / 0 is clearly shown in the right columns.
Second prisoners line with 'Bocardo' (commonly used term and visible here). Amount 0 / 10 / 0 is clear and aligned.
Latin 'Pauperibus' clearly written. Amount of 1 / 10 / 0 is distinct and correctly placed in columns.
Description heavily worn; read as 'Bill Coloni' or 'Bill Coleti' (uncertain). Amount to right reads 0 / 2 / 0 (two shillings) but the handwriting for the label is faint.
There is a heavily struck-through / overwritten line near the foot of the list with no clear descriptive text and no visible amounts; ink has been scribbled over. Marked low confidence.
Final total 'Sum - 39 5 6' clearly written at bottom-right. Numerals are crisp and used as alignment reference for the ledger columns.
